content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs (new, ~600 LOC) and its mod.rs registration. The `build_e2e_milestone_scenario_fixture` function correctly produces a self-contained Ready-state milestone workspace with plan artifacts, a realistic .beads graph (root epic + 2 task beads with parent-child and blocks dependencies), and MockBr/MockBv adapters pre-loaded with responses that match the staged bead graph. The smoke test covers all acceptance criteria: creation time (<1s verified by Instant), milestone status = Ready, plan.md/plan.json presence, total_beads = 3, persisted bundle shape (2 workstreams, 3 beads total), bead graph row count and dependency counts, and exact arg sequences emitted by BrCommand::list/show/create/close/sync_flush plus BvCommand::robot_next. Serialization roundtrips are compatible with the existing br_models deserializers (status/priority/issue_type/dependency_type/acceptance_criteria all validated). All 1064 lib tests pass; clippy and fmt are clean on the new files. The pre-existing clippy warnings elsewhere in the tree (service.rs type complexity, completion.rs items-after-test-module, etc.) are unrelated to this change. No bugs, safety, or correctness gaps found. The double materialization (MilestoneFixtureBuilder then materialize_bundle with the scenario bundle) is wasteful but functionally correct — the second call detects a plan hash change and refreshes atomically. The intentional divergence between plan bead IDs (ms-e2e-scenario.bead-N) and graph bead IDs (root-epic, task-prepare-workspace, task-validate-mocks) is a fixture design choice consistent with the stated purpose; downstream scenario tests that exercise export would drive graph creation themselves. No amendments proposed.

## Amendments

None.
